text: Community nursing is one of the main strategies used for health promotion and prevention in Ecuador, particularly in rural and vulnerable areas in urban context. This study aims to demonstrate the influence of community nurses on education, immunization and other aspects of noncommunicable diseases. An assessment that was done in one area of Quito and Tambopaxi in Guayas showed that an appropriate education given to a household could lead to improvement of health behaviours and reduction in stunting and diarrhea among children, as well as improvement in immunization coverages for children and women in marginalized urban and poor communities by means of more frequent vaccination campaigns; also, through the follow up of noncommunicable diseases in some communities it showed a better adherence to therapy and therefore an improvement in the quality of life. However, community nursing still has many challenges and needs to work on the health infrastructure, materials, capacities and thus needs policies of a public nature to achieve a change in the populations, the technological developments within the sector, also among others, the creation of alliances among the sectors involved. Therefore, based on the achievements and gaps faced by community nurses, we propose policies to strengthen and improve the efficiency of this sector to benefit health systems and communities in Ecuador and to reach the expected and necessary improvements at a social level for the citizens and to contribute to a great extent in the reduction and control of disease rates as well as to ensure their health and well-being as a great asset for nations.
